evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: with a strong focus on scalability, clarity and long-term robustness
evidence.domain_risk: Data privacy, security, and compliance knowledge
evidence.data_team_maturity: Mentor analytics engineers and analysts on modeling skills and technical standards
evidence.jd_authorship: Optimise AWS cloud-native services (Glue, Athena, S3, MWAA) to support scalable analytical and AI workloads
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Deliver high-quality semantic assets that fuel self-serve analytics, reporting and AI-powered insights
evidence.autonomy_level: Set technical direction and drive adoption of modern modeling practices across the organization
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Define and evolve the architecture for enterprise analytical models
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: 'Define and evolve' captures both establishing new architecture and iterating on it over time, supporting the mixed classification.
evidence.ai_role: Deliver high-quality semantic assets that fuel self-serve analytics, reporting and AI-powered insights
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate builds semantic layers and modeling infrastructure explicitly to power downstream AI capabilities like text-to-SQL and conversational analytics, which is data infrastructure enablement for AI consumption.
evidence.testing_framing: Mentor analytics engineers and analysts on modeling skills and technical standards
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: The JD emphasizes modeling standards, governance, and mentorship but never names a testing tool or frames testing/QA as an owned responsibility.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Data privacy, security, and compliance knowledge
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Compliance and security knowledge is listed as a core required qualification alongside repeated governance/robustness language, indicating trust and compliance concerns dominate over pure delivery speed.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Multiple rigour signals (scalability, robustness, governance, standards, compliance, quality) with zero velocity signals.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Financial domain (automotive leasing) implies higher risk, but JD emphasizes analytics architecture without explicit financial reporting or regulatory compliance language.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Multiple existing team members and established function indicate mid-stage team, with language like 'define and evolve' and 'lead implementation of AI-driven capabilities' showing scaling and new initiatives.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Named AWS services with context, specific AI capabilities (text-to-SQL, automated insights, semantic modeling), and architectural language indicate technical hiring manager authorship.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The primary audience is internal analytics consumers and self-serve users of the semantic layer; responsibilities centre on platform infrastructure, team enablement, and internal analytics capabilities rather than commercial, product, or finance stakeholders.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role is expected to set technical direction, define architecture, and lead implementation of new capabilities; the dominant verbs are 'Define', 'Set', 'Lead', and 'Drive', with no language indicating scope is set by others.
